content.full_text:
State of California 
Memorandum 
To 
: File 
From 
: 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Lynn Montgomery 
Subject: Gift of Club Membership 
Dote :July 14, 1983 
No. M-8 3-173 
An official was given a Family membership to a swimming 
club. 
If the pass is only for the official and his family 
members then the value of the gift to him is the cost of a single 
membership. 
The rest of the value is a gift to the family. 
If 
the pass entitles him to bring any 5 people, then he must report 
the entire cost of the pass because he exercises direction and 
control over it.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether an official who receives a family membership to a swimming club must report the entire gift value or only the portion attributable to the official's personal use.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: If the pass is limited to the official and family members, only the cost of a single membership must be reported as a gift to the official; the remainder is a gift to family members. If the pass allows the official to bring any 5 people, the entire cost must be reported because the official exercises direction and control over it.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Internal FPPC memorandum format (To: File, From: Lynn Montgomery) rather than formal letter. No explicit QUESTION/CONCLUSION section headers; content presented as direct analysis. Document number M-83-173 indicates this is likely a file memorandum summarizing legal analysis rather than a formal advice letter.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether an official who receives a family membership to a swimming club must report the entire gift value or only the portion attributable to the official's personal use.

CONCLUSION: If the pass is limited to the official and family members, only the cost of a single membership must be reported as a gift to the official; the remainder is a gift to family members. If the pass allows the official to bring any 5 people, the entire cost must be reported because the official exercises direction and control over it.
embedding.first_500_words: State of California Memorandum To : File From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION Lynn Montgomery Subject: Gift of Club Membership Dote :July 14, 1983 No. M-8 3-173 An official was given a Family membership to a swimming club. If the pass is only for the official and his family members then the value of the gift to him is the cost of a single membership. The rest of the value is a gift to the family. If the pass entitles him to bring any 5 people, then he must report the entire cost of the pass because he exercises direction and control over it.
embedding.summary: An FPPC memorandum analyzing the gift reporting requirements for an official who received a family membership to a swimming club, distinguishing between restricted family-only access and discretionary access to bring any individuals.
